# OneView by Skills for Change
> Capture once, report to every funder. Outcomes tracking and funder reporting platform for immigrants and refugees in Toronto.

## Purpose

OneView allows Skills for Change staff to record client data once and automatically generate compliance-ready reports for 4 funders (IRCC, Employment Ontario, United Way, City of Toronto) across 8 programs. Features include: client intake, pipeline stage tracking, outcome recording, funder CSV export, AI narrative generation, AI Q&A, exit surveys, case notes, audit logging, and program analytics.

## Base URL

https://sfc-oneview.vercel.app

---

## Authentication and Authorization

OneView supports three access roles. In the current demo deployment, roles are passed as a URL query parameter. This mechanism is a placeholder: production will replace it with JWT-based sessions and an identity provider (SSO or OAuth 2.0) so that roles are asserted server-side and cannot be spoofed.

| Role        | Demo auth method          | Permissions |
|-------------|---------------------------|-------------|
| admin       | ?role=admin               | Full access: all programs, all clients, export, reset, audit log |
| caseworker  | ?role=caseworker          | Create clients, read own program, case notes, surveys |
| viewer      | ?role=viewer              | Read-only dashboards, no PII fields returned |
| ai_agent    | Authorization: Bearer <ONEVIEW_API_KEY> | /api/query and /api/export only |

Default role when no query param is present: admin (demo convenience only; production will require explicit authentication).

AI Agent calls must include the header: `Authorization: Bearer <ONEVIEW_API_KEY>`

---

## Data Model

### clients
id (uuid, PK), full_name (text), primary_language (text), immigration_stream (text), stage (text), source (text), country_of_origin (text), age_group (text), gender (text), phone (text, nullable), email (text, nullable), created_at (timestamptz)

stage values: outreach, vetting, eligibility, intake, training, placement, survey, complete, dropped

age_group values: 18-24, 25-34, 35-44, 45-54, 55+

source values: referral, partner, walk-in, online, event, direct

immigration_stream values: refugee, skilled_worker, family, international_student, asylum_seeker, temporary_worker

### enrolments
id (uuid, PK), client_id (uuid -> clients), program (text), funder (text), consent_cross_program (bool, default false), enrolled_at (timestamptz)

### outcomes
id (uuid, PK), enrolment_id (uuid -> enrolments), tier (text), label (text), achieved (bool), recorded_at (timestamptz)

tier values: immediate, intermediate, ultimate

### surveys
id (uuid, PK), client_id (uuid -> clients), enrolment_id (uuid -> enrolments), satisfaction (int 1-5), would_recommend (bool), barriers (text, nullable), success_story (text, nullable), created_at (timestamptz)

### audit_log
id (uuid, PK), action (text), entity (text), detail (jsonb), user_role (text), source_ip (text), at (timestamptz)

### report_cache
id (uuid, PK), funder (text), period (text), narrative (text), created_at (timestamptz)

---

## Programs and Funders

| Program      | Funder | Funder full name             |
|--------------|--------|------------------------------|
| settlement   | ircc   | Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada |
| language     | ircc   | Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada |
| employment   | eo     | Employment Ontario           |
| trades       | eo     | Employment Ontario           |
| mental_health| uw     | United Way Greater Toronto   |
| youth        | uw     | United Way Greater Toronto   |
| mentoring    | city   | City of Toronto              |
| women        | city   | City of Toronto              |

---

## Pipeline Stages

Clients move through 9 stages in order:
1. outreach: Initial contact, not yet assessed
2. vetting: Background suitability check
3. eligibility: Formal eligibility determination
4. intake: Enrolled in program (active client)
5. training: Receiving services
6. placement: Placed in job, housing, or community role
7. survey: Services complete, awaiting exit survey
8. complete: Journey finished with survey submitted
9. dropped: Exited without completing program

---

## Outcome Tiers and Labels by Program

Each enrolment has three outcomes, one per tier. Achieved = true means the outcome was met.

settlement: immediate="Needs assessment completed", intermediate="Service plan activated", ultimate="Settlement goals achieved"
employment: immediate="Skills gap identified", intermediate="Job search skills built", ultimate="Employment secured"
language: immediate="Language assessment done", intermediate="Language benchmark improved", ultimate="Language goal reached"
mental_health: immediate="Mental health screen done", intermediate="Coping strategies learned", ultimate="Wellbeing improved"
trades: immediate="Trade aptitude assessed", intermediate="Certification earned", ultimate="Trades employment secured"
mentoring: immediate="Mentor matched", intermediate="Milestone check-in completed", ultimate="Mentorship goals met"
youth: immediate="Youth intake completed", intermediate="Life skills developed", ultimate="Education or employment achieved"
women: immediate="Safety plan created", intermediate="Skills training completed", ultimate="Economic independence achieved"

---

## Privacy Rules

### PHI Wall
mental_health program data is ALWAYS excluded from cross-program queries regardless of consent. This is a PHIPA hard requirement enforced at the SQL layer on every cross-program query:
  AND e.program != 'mental_health'
No role, API key, or consent flag can override this.

### Consent Levels
1. Program consent (automatic): client data used only for that program's funder reporting
2. Cross-program consent (explicit opt-in): consent_cross_program=true in enrolments; required for aggregate cross-program dashboards
3. Dashboard aggregation (implied): anonymized aggregate metrics only, no PII

### Applicable Privacy Laws by Program
- settlement, language: Federal Privacy Act
- employment, trades: FIPPA (Ontario)
- mental_health: PHIPA (PHI Wall always active)
- youth: CYFSA Part X
- mentoring, women: MFIPPA (City of Toronto)

---

## API Reference

### GET /api/health
Returns platform status and DB connectivity.
Response: {status:"ok", db:true, version:"1.0.0"}
Auth: Public

### GET /api/init
Initializes database schema and seeds demo data if tables are empty.
Response: {initialized:true, seeded:boolean, clientCount:number}
Auth: Public
Note: Seeds 16,247 demo clients across all 8 programs with realistic distributions.

### GET /api/migrate
Runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for all 6 tables. Safe to call on an existing database.
Response: {migrated:true}
Auth: Public

### GET /api/clients
Returns all clients with enrolment details and outcome summary metrics.
Query params: program (filter by program), stage (filter by stage)
Response: {clients:[{id, full_name, primary_language, immigration_stream, stage, source, country_of_origin, age_group, gender, program, funder, consent_cross_program, enrolled_at, outcomes_achieved, outcomes_total}], metrics:{total, active, crossProgram, outcomesAchievedPct, byProgram}}
Auth: Caseworker+

### POST /api/clients
Create a new client with enrolment and default outcomes.
Body: {full_name:string, primary_language:string, immigration_stream:string, program:string, funder?:string, consent_cross_program?:boolean, stage?:string, source?:string, country_of_origin?:string, age_group?:string, gender?:string, phone?:string, email?:string}
Response: {clientId:string, enrolId:string} (201)
Auth: Caseworker+

### POST /api/export
Generate a funder-specific compliance CSV for upload to funder portals.
Body: {funder:"ircc"|"eo"|"uw"|"city"}
Response: {headers:string[], rows:string[][], funder:string, label:string, count:number}
Auth: Admin

### POST /api/draft-report
Generate an AI narrative for a funder period report. Checks cache first; use forceLive:true to bypass.
Body: {funder:string, period:string, forceLive?:boolean}
Response: {narrative:string, cached:boolean, metrics:object}
Auth: Admin

### POST /api/query
Ask a natural language question about program metrics. AI answer grounded in live database metrics.
Body: {question:string}
Response: {answer:string, metrics:object}
Auth: Caseworker+ or AI Agent (Bearer token)

### POST /api/reset
Truncates all tables and re-seeds 16,247 demo clients. Destructive.
Body: {}
Response: {success:true, message:string}
Auth: Admin

### GET /api/provider
Returns the currently configured LLM provider.
Response: {provider:"gemini"|"claude"|"groq"}
Auth: Public

### GET /api/analytics
Returns program analytics, survey statistics, and outcome trends.
Response: {programs:[{program, funder, enrolled, outcomesAchievedPct, satisfaction}], surveys:{total, avgSatisfaction, wouldRecommendPct}, outcomesByTier:{immediate, intermediate, ultimate}, ageDistribution:object, genderDistribution:object}
Auth: Caseworker+

### GET /api/pipeline
Returns client counts by pipeline stage across all programs.
Response: {stages:[{stage, count}], total:number}
Auth: Caseworker+

### GET /api/journey
Returns a full client journey including enrolment, outcomes, case notes, and survey.
Query params: clientId (required)
Response: {client:object, enrolment:object, outcomes:[object], notes:[object], survey:object|null}
Auth: Caseworker+

### GET /api/pending-surveys
Returns clients in the "survey" pipeline stage who have not yet submitted an exit survey.
Response: {clients:[{id, full_name, program, enrolled_at, surveyUrl}], count:number}
Auth: Caseworker+

### POST /api/survey
Submit an exit survey for a client. Moves client stage to "complete".
Body: {clientId:string, enrolmentId:string, satisfaction:number(1-5), would_recommend:boolean, barriers?:string, success_story?:string}
Response: {surveyId:string} (201)
Auth: Public (survey links are sent to clients directly)

### GET /api/survey/[id]
Retrieve a submitted survey result for a specific client.
Response: {survey:{id, satisfaction, would_recommend, barriers, success_story, created_at}}
Auth: Caseworker+

### GET /api/notes
Retrieve case notes for a client.
Query params: clientId (required)
Response: {notes:[{id, content, user_role, created_at}]}
Auth: Caseworker+

### POST /api/notes
Add a case note for a client.
Body: {clientId:string, content:string}
Response: {noteId:string} (201)
Auth: Caseworker+

### GET /api/audit
Retrieve audit log entries, most recent first.
Query params: limit (default 100), entity (filter by entity type)
Response: {entries:[{id, action, entity, detail, user_role, source_ip, at}]}
Auth: Admin

### GET /api/ai-insights
Generate AI-powered program insights. Falls back to 5 rule-based statistical comparisons if no LLM key is configured: best program vs org average outcome rate, programs below average, top intake channel by volume, and client satisfaction vs the 3.8 sector benchmark.
Response: {insights:[{title:string, body:string, source:"llm"|"rule-based"}]}
Auth: Caseworker+

---

## Environment Variables

LLM_PROVIDER: gemini (default) | claude | groq
LLM_API_KEY: API key for the selected LLM provider
POSTGRES_URL: Vercel Postgres connection string (auto-injected on Vercel)
POSTGRES_PRISMA_URL: Pooled Postgres connection
POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING: Direct (non-pooled) Postgres connection
POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_HOST, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_DATABASE: Individual connection params
ONEVIEW_API_KEY: Bearer token granting ai_agent role access to /api/query and /api/export

---

## Survey Flow

When a client reaches the "survey" stage:
1. GET /api/pending-surveys returns the client with a pre-constructed survey URL
2. Caseworker sends the link (email or SMS) to the client
3. Client opens /survey/[clientId] and fills out the form (no login required)
4. POST /api/survey records the response and patches the client stage to "complete"
5. GET /api/journey?clientId=... returns the full journey including the survey result

---

## AI Capabilities

POST /api/query: Ask anything about program metrics in plain English. Examples:
- "How many clients completed employment in the last 90 days?"
- "What is the outcome rate for trades compared to the organization average?"
- "Which funder has the highest satisfaction score?"

POST /api/draft-report: Generates a formal narrative suitable for submission to a funder. Includes outcome rates, satisfaction, cross-program summary (PHI Wall applied), and period comparison.

GET /api/ai-insights: Proactive insights highlighting performance outliers, trends, and areas of concern.

---

## Integration Notes

### Microsoft Power Automate
Configure an HTTP action on form submission to POST /api/clients. Field mapping:
- Full Name -> full_name
- Language -> primary_language
- Immigration Background -> immigration_stream
- Program -> program
- Consent Shared -> consent_cross_program (boolean)

### Excel / SharePoint
Export historical records as CSV. Map columns to OneView schema. Bulk import via repeated POST /api/clients calls.

### iCARE (IRCC portal)
Generate CSV: POST /api/export {funder:"ircc"}. Upload the downloaded CSV manually to the iCARE portal.

### EOIS-CaMS (Employment Ontario portal)
Generate CSV: POST /api/export {funder:"eo"}. Upload manually.

### United Way ETO
Generate CSV: POST /api/export {funder:"uw"}. Upload manually.

### City of Toronto e-Service
Generate CSV: POST /api/export {funder:"city"}. Upload manually.

---

## Demo Data

Seeded on /api/init with 16,247 clients:
- settlement: 2,847 clients
- employment: 2,203 clients
- language: 2,156 clients
- mental_health: 1,891 clients
- trades: 1,644 clients
- mentoring: 1,956 clients
- youth: 1,789 clients
- women: 1,761 clients

Distributions are deterministic and realistic: age skews young (18-34 = 75%), gender reflects newcomer population diversity, source weighted toward referrals and partner organizations, intake dates follow a power-curve with recent months heavier, outcome rates reflect a high-performing organization (72-97% by program and tier).
